name: Indofood Agri Resources Ltd.
summary: Indofood Agri Resources Ltd. operates as a vertically integrated agribusiness company. The company engages in the research and development; oil palm seed breeding; oil palm plantations cultivation; crude palm oil production and refining; rubber sugar cane, cocoa, and tea cultivation; and industrial timber plantation activities. It operates in two segments, Plantations; and Edible Oils and Fats. The Plantations segment is involved in the development and maintenance of oil palm, rubber, and sugar cane plantations; and other business activities relating to processing, marketing, and selling palm oil, rubber, and sugar cane. The Edible Oils and Fats segment produces, markets, and sells edible oil, margarine, and shortening and other related products, as well as its derivative products. The company offers cooking oil under the Bimoli, Bimoli Spesial, Delima, and Happy brands; and margarine and shortening products under the Palmia, Simas, Amanda, Malinda, and Delima brands. As of December 31, 2019, it had a planted area of approximately 302,372 hectares, including 251,819 hectares of oil palm; 16,796 hectares of rubber; 13,543 hectares of sugar cane; and 20,214 hectares of other crops in Indonesia, as well as 85,969 hectares of planted sugar cane in Brazil. The company also owns and operates bulking facilities; provides transportation, investment, management, and technical services; and operates in the macronutrients mix fertilizers and prefabrication industries. In addition, it is involved in the fishing, trading, and industrial activities; and provision of agency services. Indofood Agri Resources Ltd. has operations in Indonesia, Singapore, China, Nigeria, Malaysia, the Philippines, Timor Leste, South Korea, and internationally. The company was incorporated in 2001 and is headquartered in Singapore. Indofood Agri Resources Ltd. is a subsidiary of Indofood Singapore Holdings Pte. Ltd.
